---
title: "Blushing Peat Moss (Sphagnum molle)"
slug: "sphagnum-molle"
group: "Plantae"
image: "https://inaturalist-open-data.s3.amazonaws.com/photos/474862135/large.jpg"        # keep for thumbnails
image-alt: "Blushing Peat Moss"  # keep for a11y
tax_kingdom: "Plantae"
tax_class: "Sphagnopsida"
tax_order: "Sphagnales"
tax_family: "Sphagnaceae"
categories: ["Plantae", "Sphagnopsida", "Sphagnales", "Sphagnaceae"]   # these drive the chips + filtering
freeze: true
---

<figure class="species-figure">

<img src="https://inaturalist-open-data.s3.amazonaws.com/photos/474862135/large.jpg" alt="Blushing Peat Moss" class="species-photo"/>

<figcaption class="photo-credit">

Photo: <a href="https://www.inaturalist.org/people/scarnevale">
scarnevale</a> (CC BY-NC) via <a href="https://www.inaturalist.org/observations/264432278">iNaturalist</a>

</figcaption>

</figure>

## Taxonomy

-   **Class:** Sphagnopsida
-   **Order:** Sphagnales
-   **Family:** Sphagnaceae

## Occurrence at DeLuca Preserve

-   [DeLuca Bio Blitz Species Observations](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?project_id=191727&subview=map&taxon_id=120146)

## Conservation & rarity context

-   IUCN: Not Listed
-   **Rarity note:** Very rare species in Florida found in acidic, saturated habitats such as bogs, seepage areas, and wet flatwoods.

## Natural history notes

A soft, spongy moss characterized by its pale green to pinkish hues that can give it a “blushing” appearance. In Florida, it is found in acidic, saturated habitats such as bogs, seepage areas, and wet flatwoods. It grows year-round in suitable conditions and plays an important role in water retention and peat formation in wetland ecosystems.

## References / links

-   [iNaturalist species page](https://www.inaturalist.org/taxa/120146-Sphagnum-molle)
-   [Local profile](https://florida.plantatlas.usf.edu/genus/1623)
